DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL H. BONNER, TEL. NO. 1-415-394-8090 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK RAWLINSON OF THE LATHAM & WATKINS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-650-463-3076 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RICHARD CASHIN, ONE EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, ONE EQUITY PARTNERS IV, L.P., and OEP II PARTNERS CO-INVEST, L.P.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED in its entirety. All four causes of action are adequately alleged. The allegations in the first amended complaint regarding the terms of Mr. Gessow's compensation do not contradict those in the original complaint. Amid v. Hawthorne Community Medical Group (1989) 212 Cal. App.3d 1383, 1390 is distinguishable because in that case the appellant "failed to allege an express non-disclosure term in four earlier complaints and having adopted a position in his prior complaint that there was no such express non-disclosure term, appellant alleged an explicit oral contract of non-disclosure [in his fourth amended complaint.]" There are no such contradictory allegations here. The compensation terms are sufficiently definite because they provide a basis for determining the existence of a breach and for giving an appropriate remedy. (Moncada v. West Coast Quartz Corp. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 768, 777.) The first cause of action for breach of contract is sufficient to overcome the doctrine of unenforceability of indefinite prmises because it alleges entitlement by Mr. Gessow to minimum compensation. Because the first cause of action for breach of oral contract is properly alleged, defendants' arguments as to the second and third causes of action for quantum meruit and breach of the implied covenant, which are dependent on the absence of a valid contract claim, lack merit. Mr. Gessow sufficiently alleges that Mr. Cashin had fraudulent intent because he did not intend to perform under the agreement at the time it was made. (FAC 36-37.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of All Specially Appearing Defendants For Reconsideration Of December 8, 2015 Order (Or Renewal) (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 1008(A),(B)), Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Michael Bonner telephone number 415-394-8090 for Plaintiff.
James Lynch of Latham & Watkins LLP telephone number 415-391-0600 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
In Re: DEFENDANTS RICHARD CASHIN AND ONE EQUITY PARTNERS IV, L.P'S Motion For Reconsideration Of December 8, 2015 Order (Or Renewal) (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 1008(A),(B)), Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Proceedings - The court grants the motion for reconsideration and reaffirms its December 8, 2015, order to continue the motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction to give Plaintiff the opportunity to conduct discovery on the issue of jurisdiction so that Plaintiff can determine which entity or entities extended the alleged oral promise for compensation to Plaintiff. ?(See Tuchscher Development Enterprises, Inc. v. San Diego Unified Port Dist. (App. 4 Dist. 2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1219 (If the requirements for reconsideration are met but the court is not persuaded the earlier ruling was erroneous, the proper course is to grant reconsideration and to reaffirm the earlier ruling).) The fact that Defendants OEP II and OEP IV consented to jurisdiction in New York after the November 10, 2015, hearing presents a new fact which supports the court granting the motion for reconsideration. (See Roulier v. Cannondale (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 1180.) The court, however, reaffirms its December 8, 2015, order to continue the motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction to allow for jurisdictional discovery. If One Equity Partners, LLC, One Equity Partners IV, L.P., and OEP II Partners Co-Invest, L.P. were involved in the oral promise allegedly made by Defendant Richard Cashin to Plaintiff and if those entities made contacts with Plaintiff and California as Plaintiff performed his obligations under the agreement, Plaintiff should be able to litigate against those entities in California. Thus, in accordance with the December 8, 2105, order Plaintiff is allowed to depose Mr. Richard Cashin on the issue of jurisdiction and Plaintiff is permitted to serve jurisdiction interrogatories on Defendants One Equity Partners, LLC, One Equity Partners IV, L.P., and OEP II Partners Co-Invest, L.P. The hearing on the motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is continued to March 21, 2016. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
